name: Editor Control
description: Generate editable forms from C# records with automatic field rendering

content.title: Editor Control
content.content:
# Editor Control

## Purpose

The Editor control generates editable forms from C# records. It automatically renders appropriate input fields based on property types and attributes.

## Basic Usage

### Example 1: Simple Editor

```csharp --render EditorExample1 --show-code
public record Calculator
{
    [Description("This is the X value")]
    public double X { get; init; }
    
    [Description("This is the Y value")]
    public double Y { get; init; }
}

Mesh.Edit(new Calculator())
```

**What you see above**:
- An `EditorControl` with 2 areas
- Each area contains a `NumberFieldControl` for the double properties
- Each field displays its `Description` attribute as help text
- Each field displays a label derived from the property name

Try editing the values in the form.

### Example 2: Editor with Result Display

```csharp --render EditorExample2 --show-code
public record Calculator
{
    [Description("This is the X value")]
    public double X { get; init; }
    
    [Description("This is the Y value")]
    public double Y { get; init; }
}

Mesh.Edit(
    new Calculator(), 
    c => Controls.Markdown($"Sum: {c.X + c.Y}")
)
```

**What you see above**:
- A `StackControl` containing:
  - `EditorControl` (as above)
  - `MarkdownControl` displaying the sum
- When you update X to 1: markdown displays "Sum: 1"
- When you update X to 2: markdown displays "Sum: 2"
- The result updates reactively as you change field values

## Property Type Mapping

| Property Type | Rendered Control | Example |
|--------------|------------------|----------|
| `double`, `int`, `decimal` | `NumberFieldControl` | X, Y in Calculator |
| `string` | `TextFieldControl` | Name, Email fields |
| `DateTime` | `DateControl` | BirthDate field |
| `bool` | `CheckBoxControl` | IsActive flag |
| `enum` | `SelectControl` | Status dropdown |

## Attributes

| Attribute | Effect |
|-----------|--------|
| `[Description("text")]` | Shows help text below field |
| `[Required]` | Field cannot be empty |
| `[StringLength(100)]` | Limits text input length |
| `[Range(0, 100)]` | Restricts number range |
| `[Dimension<T>]` | Renders dimension selector |
| `[UiControl<T>]` | Overrides default control type |

## Data Updates

**Update field value**:
```csharp
area.UpdatePointer(
    newValue,      // Value to set
    dataContext,   // Editor's data context
    new("x")       // JSON pointer path
);
```

**Result**: The editor field updates and any result controls re-render with the new data.

## Control Structure

When you call `Edit()`, MeshWeaver creates:

```
StackControl (if result specified)
тФЬтФАтФА EditorControl
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА Area 1 (property "X")
тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА NumberFieldControl
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА Area 2 (property "Y")
тФВ       тФФтФАтФА NumberFieldControl
тФФтФАтФА Result Control (optional)
    тФФтФАтФА Your specified control
```

Each property becomes an area within the EditorControl. The area contains the appropriate field control for that property's type.

## Usage Pattern

```csharp --render EditorExample3 --show-code
public record MyData
{
    [Required]
    [Description("Enter your name")]
    public string Name { get; init; } = string.Empty;
    
    public int Count { get; init; }
}

Mesh.Edit(
    new MyData(),
    data => Controls.Label($"Hello {data.Name}, count: {data.Count}")
)
```

Try typing your name and changing the count value to see the label update.
